name: doc-generator
description: Generate markdown documentation from Python codebases by analyzing source files, extracting docstrings, type hints, and code structure. Use when the user asks to document Python code, create API docs, or generate README files from source code.
capabilities: Extract module-level docstrings and class definitions from Python files
Parse function signatures with type hints and parameter descriptions
Generate structured markdown with classes, methods, and functions organized hierarchically
Process single files or entire directories recursively
Support Google, NumPy, and Sphinx docstring formats
Create usage examples from docstring content
author: bossjones
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation guide with no executable code. All 27 static findings are false positives from markdown code examples and documentation formatting. The skill describes using Python standard library tools for documentation generation with no network access or command execution.
valueStatement: Creating API documentation manually is time consuming and becomes outdated quickly. This skill automatically generates comprehensive markdown documentation from Python source code by extracting docstrings, type hints, and code structure into well organized reference material.
userTitle: Generate Python Documentation from Source Code
limitations: Requires Python source files with valid syntax to parse successfully
Quality depends on existing docstrings in the source code
Does not generate documentation for code without docstrings beyond signatures

useCases.title: API Reference Generation
useCases.description: Software engineers building Python libraries can automatically generate API reference documentation from their codebase with all public classes, methods, and functions documented in a consistent format.

useCases.targetUser: Documentation writers and open source maintainers
useCases.title: Project README Creation
useCases.description: Technical writers and project maintainers can quickly create comprehensive README files with usage examples and API documentation extracted directly from source code docstrings.

useCases.targetUser: Engineering teams working with legacy systems
useCases.title: Legacy Code Documentation
useCases.description: Development teams inheriting undocumented codebases can generate initial documentation structure from existing code signatures and any available docstrings as a starting point.

promptTemplates.title: Document a Single Module
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate documentation for the Python file src/api/handlers.py
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating docs for a specific module with API handlers

promptTemplates.title: Document Entire Package
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate comprehensive API documentation for all Python files in the src/services directory
promptTemplates.scenario: Building complete reference documentation for a service layer

promptTemplates.title: Extract Class Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Document all classes in models.py with their methods and attributes
promptTemplates.scenario: Focusing documentation on data models and their interfaces

promptTemplates.title: Generate README with Examples
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a README.md file from the main.py module including usage examples from docstrings
promptTemplates.scenario: Building project README from code examples embedded in docstrings

bestPractices: Ensure your Python source files have comprehensive docstrings before running documentation generation for best results
Use consistent docstring format throughout your codebase such as Google style or NumPy style for uniform output
Specify whether to include private methods with leading underscores if you need internal API documentation
antiPatterns: Do not expect detailed documentation from code without docstrings as the output will only contain signatures
Avoid running on files with syntax errors as they will be skipped with warnings
Do not use this for languages other than Python as it relies on Python AST parsing

faq.question: What docstring formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports Google style, NumPy style, and Sphinx style docstrings commonly used in Python projects.

faq.question: Can this generate documentation for code without docstrings?
faq.answer: It will extract function and method signatures with type hints, but descriptions will show as not provided without docstrings.

faq.question: Does this work with Python 2 code?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on modern Python 3 codebases with type hints and uses current AST parsing which may not support legacy Python 2 syntax.

faq.question: Can I customize the markdown output format?
faq.answer: The skill generates standard markdown structure with modules, classes, and functions. You can request specific sections or organization in your prompt.

faq.question: How are private methods handled?
faq.answer: Methods and functions with leading underscores are skipped by default unless you explicitly request documentation for private members.

faq.question: Will this modify my source code files?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads source files to extract documentation. It generates new markdown files without modifying the original Python code.
